     1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
     2	pragma solidity ^0.8.34;
     3	
     4	import {Test} from "forge-std/Test.sol";
     5	import {ClanWorld} from "../src/ClanWorld.sol";
     6	import {
     7	    IClanWorldEvents,
     8	    ClanWorldConstants,
     9	    Clan,
    10	    ClanOrder,
    11	    OrderResult,
    12	    StatusCode,
    13	    ActionType
    14	} from "../src/IClanWorld.sol";
    15	
    16	contract WallUpgradeHarness is ClanWorld {
    17	    function setVault(uint32 clanId, uint256 wood, uint256 iron, uint256 wheat, uint256 fish) external {
    18	        _clans[clanId].vaultWood = wood;
    19	        _clans[clanId].vaultIron = iron;
    20	        _clans[clanId].vaultWheat = wheat;
    21	        _clans[clanId].vaultFish = fish;
    22	    }
    23	}
    24	
    25	contract WallUpgradesTest is Test {
    26	    WallUpgradeHarness world;
    27	    address elder = address(0xA1);
    28	    address elder2 = address(0xA2);
    29	
    30	    function setUp() public {
    31	        world = new WallUpgradeHarness();
    32	    }
    33	
    34	    function _mintClan(address owner) internal returns (uint32 clanId) {
    35	        vm.prank(owner);
    36	        (clanId,) = world.mintClan(owner);
    37	    }
    38	
    39	    function _firstCs(uint32 clanId) internal view returns (uint32) {
    40	        return world.getClanFullView(clanId).clansmen[0].clansman.clansman.clansmanId;
    41	    }
    42	
    43	    function _csAt(uint32 clanId, uint256 index) internal view returns (uint32) {
    44	        return world.getClanFullView(clanId).clansmen[index].clansman.clansman.clansmanId;
    45	    }
    46	
    47	    function _submitOrder(address owner, uint32 clanId, uint32 csId, ActionType action)
    48	        internal
    49	        returns (OrderResult[] memory)
    50	    {
    51	        Clan memory clan = world.getClan(clanId);
    52	        ClanOrder[] memory orders = new ClanOrder[](1);
    53	        orders[0] = ClanOrder({
    54	            clansmanId: csId,
    55	            gotoRegion: clan.baseRegion,
    56	            action: action,
    57	            targetClanId: 0,
    58	            marketToken: address(0),
    59	            marketAmount: 0,
    60	            maxGoldIn: 0
    61	        });
    62	        vm.prank(owner);
    63	        return world.submitClanOrders(clanId, orders);
    64	    }
    65	
    66	    function _submitUpgradeBatch(address owner, uint32 clanId, uint256 count) internal returns (OrderResult[] memory) {
    67	        Clan memory clan = world.getClan(clanId);
    68	        ClanOrder[] memory orders = new ClanOrder[](count);
    69	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < count; i++) {
    70	            orders[i] = ClanOrder({
    71	                clansmanId: _csAt(clanId, i),
    72	                gotoRegion: clan.baseRegion,
    73	                action: ActionType.UpgradeWall,
    74	                targetClanId: 0,
    75	                marketToken: address(0),
    76	                marketAmount: 0,
    77	                maxGoldIn: 0
    78	            });
    79	        }
    80	        vm.prank(owner);
    81	        return world.submitClanOrders(clanId, orders);
    82	    }
    83	
    84	    function _advanceTick() internal {
    85	        vm.warp(block.timestamp + ClanWorldConstants.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SECONDS);
    86	        world.heartbeat();
    87	    }
    88	
    89	    function _advanceTicks(uint256 count) internal {
    90	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < count; i++) {
    91	            _advanceTick();
    92	        }
    93	    }
    94	
    95	    function test_getWallUpgradeCost_matchesSpecTable() public view {
    96	        (uint256 wood, uint256 iron) = world.getWallUpgradeCost(0);
    97	        assertEq(wood, 20e18, "level 1 wood");
    98	        assertEq(iron, 0, "level 1 iron");
    99	
   100	        (wood, iron) = world.getWallUpgradeCost(2);
   101	        assertEq(wood, 30e18, "level 3 wood");
   102	        assertEq(iron, 5e18, "level 3 iron");
   103	
   104	        (wood, iron) = world.getWallUpgradeCost(4);
   105	        assertEq(wood, 50e18, "level 5 wood");
   106	        assertEq(iron, 15e18, "level 5 iron");
   107	    }
   108	
   109	    function test_upgradeWall_deductsAtQueueAndSettlesLevel() public {
   110	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan(elder);
   111	        uint32 csId = _firstCs(clanId);
   112	        world.setVault(clanId, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18);
   113	
   114	        (uint256 woodCost, uint256 ironCost) = world.getWallUpgradeCost(0);
   115	        OrderResult[] memory result = _submitOrder(elder, clanId, csId, ActionType.UpgradeWall);
   116	
   117	        assertEq(uint8(result[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "queue status");
   118	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).vaultWood, 100e18 - woodCost, "wood deducted at queue");
   119	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).vaultIron, 100e18 - ironCost, "iron deducted at queue");
   120	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).wallLevel, 0, "wall level waits for settle");
   121	
   122	        _advanceTicks(world.getActionDuration(ActionType.UpgradeWall));
   123	        vm.expectEmit(true, false, false, true, address(world));
   124	        emit IClanWorldEvents.WallUpgraded(clanId, 1, 2);
   125	        _advanceTick();
   126	
   127	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).wallLevel, 1, "wall level after settle");
   128	    }
   129	
   130	    function test_upgradeWall_rejectsInsufficientVaultAtQueueTime() public {
   131	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan(elder);
   132	        uint32 csId = _firstCs(clanId);
   133	        world.setVault(clanId, 0, 0, 100e18, 100e18);
   134	
   135	        OrderResult[] memory result = _submitOrder(elder, clanId, csId, ActionType.UpgradeWall);
   136	
   137	        assertEq(uint8(result[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES), "missing resources");
   138	        assertFalse(world.getActiveMission(csId).active, "no mission queued");
   139	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).wallLevel, 0, "wall unchanged");
   140	    }
   141	
   142	    function test_upgradeWall_rejectsAboveMaxLevel() public {
   143	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan(elder);
   144	        world.setVault(clanId, 300e18, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18);
   145	
   146	        OrderResult[] memory firstFour = _submitUpgradeBatch(elder, clanId, 4);
   147	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
   148	            assertEq(uint8(firstFour[i].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "first batch status");
   149	        }
   150	        _advanceTicks(world.getActionDuration(ActionType.UpgradeWall) + 1);
   151	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).wallLevel, 4, "first four upgrades settled");
   152	
   153	        _advanceTicks(3);
   154	        uint32 csId = _firstCs(clanId);
   155	        OrderResult[] memory fifth = _submitOrder(elder, clanId, csId, ActionType.UpgradeWall);
   156	        assertEq(uint8(fifth[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "fifth status");
   157	        _advanceTicks(world.getActionDuration(ActionType.UpgradeWall) + 1);
   158	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).wallLevel, 5, "max wall level");
   159	
   160	        _advanceTicks(3);
   161	        OrderResult[] memory sixth = _submitOrder(elder, clanId, csId, ActionType.UpgradeWall);
   162	        assertEq(uint8(sixth[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.ERR_INVALID_ACTION), "max level rejects");
   163	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).wallLevel, 5, "wall remains max");
   164	    }
   165	
   166	    function test_upgradeWall_twoClansDoNotInterfere() public {
   167	        uint32 clanA = _mintClan(elder);
   168	        vm.prank(elder2);
   169	        (uint32 clanB,) = world.mintClan(elder2);
   170	        world.setVault(clanA, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18);
   171	        world.setVault(clanB, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18);
   172	
   173	        OrderResult[] memory result = _submitOrder(elder, clanA, _firstCs(clanA), ActionType.UpgradeWall);
   174	        assertEq(uint8(result[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "queue status");
   175	        _advanceTicks(world.getActionDuration(ActionType.UpgradeWall) + 1);
   176	
   177	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanA).wallLevel, 1, "clan A wall");
   178	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanB).wallLevel, 0, "clan B wall");
   179	    }
   180	}

     4	import {Test} from "forge-std/Test.sol";

    16	contract BaseUpgradeHarness is ClanWorld {
    17	    function setVault(uint32 clanId, uint256 wood, uint256 iron, uint256 wheat, uint256 fish) external {
    18	        _clans[clanId].vaultWood = wood;
    19	        _clans[clanId].vaultIron = iron;
    20	        _clans[clanId].vaultWheat = wheat;
    21	        _clans[clanId].vaultFish = fish;
    22	    }
    23	}
    24	
    25	contract BaseUpgradesTest is Test {
    26	    BaseUpgradeHarness world;
    27	    address elder = address(0xA1);
    28	    address elder2 = address(0xA2);
    29

    32	    }
    33	
    34	    function _mintClan(address owner) internal returns (uint32 clanId) {
    35	        vm.prank(owner);
    36	        (clanId,) = world.mintClan(owner);
    37	    }
    38	
    39	    function _firstCs(uint32 clanId) internal view returns (uint32) {
    40	        return world.getClanFullView(clanId).clansmen[0].clansman.clansman.clansmanId;
    41	    }
    42	
    43	    function _csAt(uint32 clanId, uint256 index) internal view returns (uint32) {
    44	        return world.getClanFullView(clanId).clansmen[index].clansman.clansman.clansmanId;
    45	    }
    46	
    47	    function _submitOrder(address owner, uint32 clanId, uint32 csId, ActionType action)
    48	        internal
    49	        returns (OrderResult[] memory)
    50	    {
    51	        Clan memory clan = world.getClan(clanId);
    52	        ClanOrder[] memory orders = new ClanOrder[](1);
    53	        orders[0] = ClanOrder({
    54	            clansmanId: csId,
    55	            gotoRegion: clan.baseRegion,
    56	            action: action,
    57	            targetClanId: 0,
    58	            marketToken: address(0),
    59	            marketAmount: 0,
    60	            maxGoldIn: 0
    61	        });
    62	        vm.prank(owner);
    63	        return world.submitClanOrders(clanId, orders);
    64	    }
    65	
    66	    function _submitUpgradeBatch(address owner, uint32 clanId, uint256 count) internal returns (OrderResult[] memory) {
    67	        Clan memory clan = world.getClan(clanId);
    68	        ClanOrder[] memory orders = new ClanOrder[](count);
    69	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < count; i++) {
    70	            orders[i] = ClanOrder({
    71	                clansmanId: _csAt(clanId, i),
    72	                gotoRegion: clan.baseRegion,
    73	                action: ActionType.UpgradeBase,
    74	                targetClanId: 0,
    75	                marketToken: address(0),
    76	                marketAmount: 0,
    77	                maxGoldIn: 0
    78	            });
    79	        }
    80	        vm.prank(owner);
    81	        return world.submitClanOrders(clanId, orders);
    82	    }
    83	
    84	    function _advanceTick() internal {
    85	        vm.warp(block.timestamp + ClanWorldConstants.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SECONDS);
    86	        world.heartbeat();
    87	    }
    88	
    89	    function _advanceTicks(uint256 count) internal {
    90	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < count; i++) {
    91	            _advanceTick();
    92	        }
    93	    }
    94	
    95	    function test_getBaseUpgradeCost_matchesSpecTable() public view {
    96	        (uint256 wood, uint256 iron, uint256 wheat) = world.getBaseUpgradeCost(1);
    97	        assertEq(wood, 40e18, "level 2 wood");
    98	        assertEq(iron, 0, "level 2 iron");
    99	        assertEq(wheat, 20e18, "level 2 wheat");
   100	
   101	        (wood, iron, wheat) = world.getBaseUpgradeCost(3);
   102	        assertEq(wood, 80e18, "level 4 wood");
   103	        assertEq(iron, 10e18, "level 4 iron");
   104	        assertEq(wheat, 40e18, "level 4 wheat");
   105	
   106	        (wood, iron, wheat) = world.getBaseUpgradeCost(4);
   107	        assertEq(wood, 100e18, "level 5 wood");
   108	        assertEq(iron, 15e18, "level 5 iron");
   109	        assertEq(wheat, 50e18, "level 5 wheat");
   110	    }
   111	
   112	    function test_upgradeBase_deductsAtQueueAndSettlesLevel() public {
   113	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan(elder);
   114	        uint32 csId = _firstCs(clanId);
   115	        world.setVault(clanId, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18);
   116	
   117	        (uint256 woodCost, uint256 ironCost, uint256 wheatCost) = world.getBaseUpgradeCost(1);
   118	        OrderResult[] memory result = _submitOrder(elder, clanId, csId, ActionType.UpgradeBase);
   119	
   120	        assertEq(uint8(result[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "queue status");
   121	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).vaultWood, 100e18 - woodCost, "wood deducted at queue");
   122	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).vaultIron, 100e18 - ironCost, "iron deducted at queue");
   123	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).vaultWheat, 100e18 - wheatCost, "wheat deducted at queue");
   124	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).baseLevel, 1, "base level waits for settle");
   125	
   126	        _advanceTicks(world.getActionDuration(ActionType.UpgradeBase));
   127	        vm.expectEmit(true, false, false, true, address(world));
   128	        emit IClanWorldEvents.BaseUpgraded(clanId, 2, 2);
   129	        _advanceTick();
   130	
   131	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).baseLevel, 2, "base level after settle");
   132	    }
   133	
   134	    function test_upgradeBase_rejectsInsufficientVaultAtQueueTime() public {
   135	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan(elder);
   136	        uint32 csId = _firstCs(clanId);
   137	        world.setVault(clanId, 0, 100e18, 0, 100e18);
   138	
   139	        OrderResult[] memory result = _submitOrder(elder, clanId, csId, ActionType.UpgradeBase);
   140	
   141	        assertEq(uint8(result[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES), "missing resources");
   142	        assertFalse(world.getActiveMission(csId).active, "no mission queued");
   143	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).baseLevel, 1, "base unchanged");
   144	    }
   145	
   146	    function test_upgradeBase_rejectsAboveMaxLevel() public {
   147	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan(elder);
   148	        world.setVault(clanId, 300e18, 100e18, 200e18, 100e18);
   149	
   150	        OrderResult[] memory firstFour = _submitUpgradeBatch(elder, clanId, 4);
   151	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
   152	            assertEq(uint8(firstFour[i].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "first batch status");
   153	        }
   154	        _advanceTicks(world.getActionDuration(ActionType.UpgradeBase) + 1);
   155	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).baseLevel, 5, "max base level");
   156	
   157	        _advanceTicks(3);
   158	        uint32 csId = _firstCs(clanId);
   159	        OrderResult[] memory fifth = _submitOrder(elder, clanId, csId, ActionType.UpgradeBase);
   160	        assertEq(uint8(fifth[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.ERR_INVALID_ACTION), "max level rejects");
   161	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).baseLevel, 5, "base remains max");
   162	    }
   163	
   164	    function test_upgradeBase_twoClansDoNotInterfere() public {
   165	        uint32 clanA = _mintClan(elder);
   166	        vm.prank(elder2);
   167	        (uint32 clanB,) = world.mintClan(elder2);
   168	        world.setVault(clanA, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18);
   169	        world.setVault(clanB, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18);
   170	
   171	        OrderResult[] memory result = _submitOrder(elder, clanA, _firstCs(clanA), ActionType.UpgradeBase);
   172	        assertEq(uint8(result[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "queue status");
   173	        _advanceTicks(world.getActionDuration(ActionType.UpgradeBase) + 1);
   174	
   175	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanA).baseLevel, 2, "clan A base");
   176	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanB).baseLevel, 1, "clan B base");
   177	    }
   178	}
     1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
     2	pragma solidity ^0.8.34;
     3	
     4	import {Test} from "forge-std/Test.sol";
     5	import {ClanWorld} from "../src/ClanWorld.sol";
     6	import {
     7	    IClanWorldEvents,
     8	    ClanWorldConstants,
     9	    Clan,
    10	    ClanOrder,
    11	    OrderResult,
    12	    StatusCode,
    13	    ActionType
    14	} from "../src/IClanWorld.sol";
    15	
    16	contract MonumentUpgradeHarness is ClanWorld {
    17	    function setVault(uint32 clanId, uint256 wood, uint256 iron, uint256 wheat, uint256 fish) external {
    18	        _clans[clanId].vaultWood = wood;
    19	        _clans[clanId].vaultIron = iron;
    20	        _clans[clanId].vaultWheat = wheat;
    21	        _clans[clanId].vaultFish = fish;
    22	    }
    23	
    24	    function setBlueprint(uint32 clanId, uint256 blueprint) external {
    25	        _clans[clanId].blueprintBalance = blueprint;
    26	    }
    27	
    28	    function setMonumentLevel(uint32 clanId, uint8 level) external {
    29	        _clans[clanId].monumentLevel = level;
    30	    }
    31	}
    32	
    33	contract MonumentUpgradesTest is Test {
    34	    MonumentUpgradeHarness world;
    35	    address elder = address(0xA1);
    36	    address elder2 = address(0xA2);
    37	
    38	    function setUp() public {
    39	        world = new MonumentUpgradeHarness();
    40	    }
    41	
    42	    function _mintClan(address owner) internal returns (uint32 clanId) {
    43	        vm.prank(owner);
    44	        (clanId,) = world.mintClan(owner);
    45	    }
    46	
    47	    function _firstCs(uint32 clanId) internal view returns (uint32) {
    48	        return world.getClanFullView(clanId).clansmen[0].clansman.clansman.clansmanId;
    49	    }
    50	
    51	    function _csAt(uint32 clanId, uint256 index) internal view returns (uint32) {
    52	        return world.getClanFullView(clanId).clansmen[index].clansman.clansman.clansmanId;
    53	    }
    54	
    55	    function _submitOrder(address owner, uint32 clanId, uint32 csId, ActionType action)
    56	        internal
    57	        returns (OrderResult[] memory)
    58	    {
    59	        Clan memory clan = world.getClan(clanId);
    60	        ClanOrder[] memory orders = new ClanOrder[](1);
    61	        orders[0] = ClanOrder({
    62	            clansmanId: csId,
    63	            gotoRegion: clan.baseRegion,
    64	            action: action,
    65	            targetClanId: 0,
    66	            marketToken: address(0),
    67	            marketAmount: 0,
    68	            maxGoldIn: 0
    69	        });
    70	        vm.prank(owner);
    71	        return world.submitClanOrders(clanId, orders);
    72	    }
    73	
    74	    function _submitUpgradeBatch(address owner, uint32 clanId, uint256 count) internal returns (OrderResult[] memory) {
    75	        Clan memory clan = world.getClan(clanId);
    76	        ClanOrder[] memory orders = new ClanOrder[](count);
    77	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < count; i++) {
    78	            orders[i] = ClanOrder({
    79	                clansmanId: _csAt(clanId, i),
    80	                gotoRegion: clan.baseRegion,
    81	                action: ActionType.UpgradeMonument,
    82	                targetClanId: 0,
    83	                marketToken: address(0),
    84	                marketAmount: 0,
    85	                maxGoldIn: 0
    86	            });
    87	        }
    88	        vm.prank(owner);
    89	        return world.submitClanOrders(clanId, orders);
    90	    }
    91	
    92	    function _advanceTick() internal {
    93	        vm.warp(block.timestamp + ClanWorldConstants.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SECONDS);
    94	        world.heartbeat();
    95	    }
    96	
    97	    function _advanceTicks(uint256 count) internal {
    98	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < count; i++) {
    99	            _advanceTick();
   100	        }
   101	    }
   102	
   103	    function test_getMonumentUpgradeCost_matchesPhaseTable() public view {
   104	        (uint256 wood, uint256 iron, uint256 wheat, uint256 blueprint) = world.getMonumentUpgradeCost(0);
   105	        assertEq(wood, 30e18, "level 1 wood");
   106	        assertEq(iron, 0, "level 1 iron");
   107	        assertEq(wheat, 20e18, "level 1 wheat");
   108	        assertEq(blueprint, 0, "level 1 blueprint");
   109	
   110	        (wood, iron, wheat, blueprint) = world.getMonumentUpgradeCost(5);
   111	        assertEq(wood, 150e18, "level 6 wood");
   112	        assertEq(iron, 20e18, "level 6 iron");
   113	        assertEq(wheat, 80e18, "level 6 wheat");
   114	        assertEq(blueprint, 0, "level 6 blueprint");
   115	
   116	        (wood, iron, wheat, blueprint) = world.getMonumentUpgradeCost(6);
   117	        assertEq(wood, 200e18, "level 7 wood");
   118	        assertEq(iron, 25e18, "level 7 iron");
   119	        assertEq(wheat, 100e18, "level 7 wheat");
   120	        assertEq(blueprint, 1e18, "level 7 blueprint");
   121	    }
   122	
   123	    function test_upgradeMonument_deductsAtQueueAndSettlesLevel() public {
   124	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan(elder);
   125	        uint32 csId = _firstCs(clanId);
   126	        world.setVault(clanId, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18);
   127	        world.setBlueprint(clanId, 5e18);
   128	
   129	        (uint256 woodCost, uint256 ironCost, uint256 wheatCost, uint256 blueprintCost) = world.getMonumentUpgradeCost(0);
   130	        OrderResult[] memory result = _submitOrder(elder, clanId, csId, ActionType.UpgradeMonument);
   131	
   132	        assertEq(uint8(result[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "queue status");
   133	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).vaultWood, 100e18 - woodCost, "wood deducted at queue");
   134	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).vaultIron, 100e18 - ironCost, "iron deducted at queue");
   135	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).vaultWheat, 100e18 - wheatCost, "wheat deducted at queue");
   136	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).blueprintBalance, 5e18 - blueprintCost, "blueprint deducted at queue");
   137	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).monumentLevel, 0, "monument level waits for settle");
   138	
   139	        _advanceTicks(world.getActionDuration(ActionType.UpgradeMonument));
   140	        vm.expectEmit(true, false, false, true, address(world));
   141	        emit IClanWorldEvents.MonumentUpgraded(clanId, 1, 4);
   142	        _advanceTick();
   143	
   144	        assertEq(world.getClanFullView(clanId).clan.clan.monumentLevel, 1, "monument level after settle");
   145	    }
   146	
   147	    function test_upgradeMonument_rejectsInsufficientVaultAtQueueTime() public {
   148	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan(elder);
   149	        uint32 csId = _firstCs(clanId);
   150	        world.setVault(clanId, 0, 100e18, 0, 100e18);
   151	
   152	        OrderResult[] memory result = _submitOrder(elder, clanId, csId, ActionType.UpgradeMonument);
   153	
   154	        assertEq(uint8(result[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES), "missing resources");
   155	        assertFalse(world.getActiveMission(csId).active, "no mission queued");
   156	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).monumentLevel, 0, "monument unchanged");
   157	    }
   158	
   159	    function test_upgradeMonument_requiresBlueprintForLateLevel() public {
   160	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan(elder);
   161	        uint32 csId = _firstCs(clanId);
   162	        world.setMonumentLevel(clanId, 6);
   163	        world.setVault(clanId, 300e18, 100e18, 200e18, 100e18);
   164	
   165	        OrderResult[] memory result = _submitOrder(elder, clanId, csId, ActionType.UpgradeMonument);
   166	
   167	        assertEq(uint8(result[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.ERR_MISSING_RESOURCES), "missing blueprint");
   168	        assertFalse(world.getActiveMission(csId).active, "no mission queued");
   169	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).monumentLevel, 6, "monument unchanged");
   170	    }
   171	
   172	    function test_upgradeMonument_rejectsAboveMaxLevel() public {
   173	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan(elder);
   174	        world.setVault(clanId, 2_000e18, 500e18, 1_000e18, 100e18);
   175	        world.setBlueprint(clanId, 4e18);
   176	
   177	        uint256 remaining = 10;
   178	        while (remaining > 0) {
   179	            uint256 batchSize = remaining > 4 ? 4 : remaining;
   180	            OrderResult[] memory batch = _submitUpgradeBatch(elder, clanId, batchSize);
   181	            for (uint256 i = 0; i < batchSize; i++) {
   182	                assertEq(uint8(batch[i].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "batch status");
   183	            }
   184	            _advanceTicks(world.getActionDuration(ActionType.UpgradeMonument) + 1);
   185	            _advanceTicks(3);
   186	            remaining -= batchSize;
   187	        }
   188	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).monumentLevel, 10, "max monument level");
   189	
   190	        uint32 csId = _firstCs(clanId);
   191	        OrderResult[] memory eleventh = _submitOrder(elder, clanId, csId, ActionType.UpgradeMonument);
   192	        assertEq(uint8(eleventh[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.ERR_INVALID_ACTION), "max level rejects");
   193	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanId).monumentLevel, 10, "monument remains max");
   194	    }
   195	
   196	    function test_upgradeMonument_twoClansDoNotInterfere() public {
   197	        uint32 clanA = _mintClan(elder);
   198	        vm.prank(elder2);
   199	        (uint32 clanB,) = world.mintClan(elder2);
   200	        world.setVault(clanA, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18);
   201	        world.setVault(clanB, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18, 100e18);
   202	
   203	        OrderResult[] memory result = _submitOrder(elder, clanA, _firstCs(clanA), ActionType.UpgradeMonument);
   204	        assertEq(uint8(result[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "queue status");
   205	        _advanceTicks(world.getActionDuration(ActionType.UpgradeMonument) + 1);
   206	
   207	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanA).monumentLevel, 1, "clan A monument");
   208	        assertEq(world.getClan(clanB).monumentLevel, 0, "clan B monument");
   209	    }
   210	}
     1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
     2	pragma solidity ^0.8.34;
     3	
     4	import {Test} from "forge-std/Test.sol";
     5	import {ClanWorld} from "../src/ClanWorld.sol";
     6	import {ClanWorldConstants, Clan, ClanOrder, OrderResult, StatusCode, ActionType} from "../src/IClanWorld.sol";
     7	
     8	contract RankGetterHarness is ClanWorld {
     9	    function setVault(uint32 clanId, uint256 wood, uint256 iron, uint256 wheat, uint256 fish) external {
    10	        _clans[clanId].vaultWood = wood;
    11	        _clans[clanId].vaultIron = iron;
    12	        _clans[clanId].vaultWheat = wheat;
    13	        _clans[clanId].vaultFish = fish;
    14	    }
    15	
    16	    function setWallLevel(uint32 clanId, uint8 wallLevel) external {
    17	        _clans[clanId].wallLevel = wallLevel;
    18	    }
    19	}
    20	
    21	contract RankGettersTest is Test {
    22	    RankGetterHarness world;
    23	    address elderA = address(0xA1);
    24	    address elderB = address(0xA2);
    25	    address elderC = address(0xA3);
    26	
    27	    function setUp() public {
    28	        world = new RankGetterHarness();
    29	    }
    30	
    31	    function _mintClan(address owner) internal returns (uint32 clanId) {
    32	        vm.prank(owner);
    33	        (clanId,) = world.mintClan(owner);
    34	    }
    35	
    36	    function _csAt(uint32 clanId, uint256 index) internal view returns (uint32) {
    37	        return world.getClanFullView(clanId).clansmen[index].clansman.clansman.clansmanId;
    38	    }
    39	
    40	    function _submitUpgradeBatch(address owner, uint32 clanId, uint256 count) internal returns (OrderResult[] memory) {
    41	        Clan memory clan = world.getClan(clanId);
    42	        ClanOrder[] memory orders = new ClanOrder[](count);
    43	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < count; i++) {
    44	            orders[i] = ClanOrder({
    45	                clansmanId: _csAt(clanId, i),
    46	                gotoRegion: clan.baseRegion,
    47	                action: ActionType.UpgradeMonument,
    48	                targetClanId: 0,
    49	                marketToken: address(0),
    50	                marketAmount: 0,
    51	                maxGoldIn: 0
    52	            });
    53	        }
    54	
    55	        vm.prank(owner);
    56	        return world.submitClanOrders(clanId, orders);
    57	    }
    58	
    59	    function _advanceTick() internal {
    60	        vm.warp(block.timestamp + ClanWorldConstants.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SECONDS);
    61	        world.heartbeat();
    62	    }
    63	
    64	    function _advanceTicks(uint256 count) internal {
    65	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < count; i++) {
    66	            _advanceTick();
    67	        }
    68	    }
    69	
    70	    function _assertAllOk(OrderResult[] memory results) internal pure {
    71	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < results.length; i++) {
    72	            assertEq(uint8(results[i].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "upgrade queued");
    73	        }
    74	    }
    75	
    76	    function _expectedScore(uint8 level, uint64 reachedAtTick, uint256 lootValue) internal pure returns (uint256) {
    77	        return _expectedScore(level, reachedAtTick, lootValue, 0);
    78	    }
    79	
    80	    function _expectedScore(uint8 level, uint64 reachedAtTick, uint256 lootValue, uint8 wallLevel)
    81	        internal
    82	        pure
    83	        returns (uint256)
    84	    {
    85	        uint256 maxLootComponent = (uint256(1) << 176) - 1;
    86	        if (lootValue > maxLootComponent) {
    87	            lootValue = maxLootComponent;
    88	        }
    89	
    90	        uint256 timeComponent;
    91	        if (level > 0) {
    92	            timeComponent = uint256(type(uint64).max) - uint256(reachedAtTick);
    93	        }
    94	
    95	        return (uint256(level) << 248) | (timeComponent << 184) | (lootValue << 8) | wallLevel;
    96	    }
    97	
    98	    function test_getClanScoreAndRankings_sortByLevelThenEarliestReachTick() public {
    99	        uint32 clanA = _mintClan(elderA);
   100	        uint32 clanB = _mintClan(elderB);
   101	        uint32 clanC = _mintClan(elderC);
   102	
   103	        world.setVault(clanA, 300e18, 100e18, 300e18, 0);
   104	        world.setVault(clanB, 300e18, 100e18, 300e18, 0);
   105	        world.setVault(clanC, 300e18, 100e18, 300e18, 0);
   106	
   107	        _assertAllOk(_submitUpgradeBatch(elderA, clanA, 2));
   108	        _assertAllOk(_submitUpgradeBatch(elderB, clanB, 1));
   109	
   110	        _advanceTick();
   111	        _assertAllOk(_submitUpgradeBatch(elderC, clanC, 1));
   112	        _advanceTicks(world.getActionDuration(ActionType.UpgradeMonument) + 1);
   113	
   114	        (uint256 scoreA, uint64 reachedA, uint8 levelA) = world.getClanScore(clanA);
   115	        (uint256 scoreB, uint64 reachedB, uint8 levelB) = world.getClanScore(clanB);
   116	        (uint256 scoreC, uint64 reachedC, uint8 levelC) = world.getClanScore(clanC);
   117	
   118	        assertEq(levelA, 2, "A level");
   119	        assertEq(levelB, 1, "B level");
   120	        assertEq(levelC, 1, "C level");
   121	        assertEq(reachedA, 4, "A reached level 2");
   122	        assertEq(reachedB, 4, "B reached level 1");
   123	        assertEq(reachedC, 5, "C reached level 1");
   124	        assertEq(scoreA, _expectedScore(levelA, reachedA, world.quoteLootValueRaw(clanA)), "A score");
   125	        assertEq(scoreB, _expectedScore(levelB, reachedB, world.quoteLootValueRaw(clanB)), "B score");
   126	        assertEq(scoreC, _expectedScore(levelC, reachedC, world.quoteLootValueRaw(clanC)), "C score");
   127	        assertGt(scoreA, scoreB, "higher monument level wins");
   128	        assertGt(scoreB, scoreC, "earlier reach tick wins");
   129	
   130	        (uint32[] memory ranked, uint256[] memory scores) = world.getRankings();
   131	        assertEq(ranked.length, 3, "ranked count");
   132	        assertEq(scores.length, 3, "score count");
   133	        assertEq(ranked[0], clanA, "rank 1");
   134	        assertEq(ranked[1], clanB, "rank 2");
   135	        assertEq(ranked[2], clanC, "rank 3");
   136	        assertEq(scores[0], scoreA, "rank 1 score");
   137	        assertEq(scores[1], scoreB, "rank 2 score");
   138	        assertEq(scores[2], scoreC, "rank 3 score");
   139	    }
   140	
   141	    function test_getRankings_breaksExactScoreTiesByClanId() public {
   142	        uint32 clanA = _mintClan(elderA);
   143	        uint32 clanB = _mintClan(elderB);
   144	        uint32 clanC = _mintClan(elderC);
   145	
   146	        world.setVault(clanA, 100e18, 0, 100e18, 0);
   147	        world.setVault(clanB, 100e18, 0, 100e18, 0);
   148	        world.setVault(clanC, 100e18, 0, 100e18, 0);
   149	
   150	        _assertAllOk(_submitUpgradeBatch(elderA, clanA, 1));
   151	        _assertAllOk(_submitUpgradeBatch(elderB, clanB, 1));
   152	        _assertAllOk(_submitUpgradeBatch(elderC, clanC, 1));
   153	        _advanceTicks(world.getActionDuration(ActionType.UpgradeMonument) + 1);
   154	
   155	        (uint256 scoreA,,) = world.getClanScore(clanA);
   156	        (uint256 scoreB,,) = world.getClanScore(clanB);
   157	        (uint256 scoreC,,) = world.getClanScore(clanC);
   158	        assertEq(scoreA, scoreB, "A/B exact tie");
   159	        assertEq(scoreB, scoreC, "B/C exact tie");
   160	
   161	        (uint32[] memory ranked, uint256[] memory scores) = world.getRankings();
   162	        assertEq(ranked.length, 3, "ranked count");
   163	        assertEq(ranked[0], clanA, "lowest clanId first");
   164	        assertEq(ranked[1], clanB, "middle clanId second");
   165	        assertEq(ranked[2], clanC, "highest clanId third");
   166	        assertEq(scores[0], scoreA, "A score");
   167	        assertEq(scores[1], scoreB, "B score");
   168	        assertEq(scores[2], scoreC, "C score");
   169	    }
   170	
   171	    function test_getRankings_usesWallLevelAfterLootBeforeClanId() public {
   172	        uint32 clanA = _mintClan(elderA);
   173	        uint32 clanB = _mintClan(elderB);
   174	        uint32 clanC = _mintClan(elderC);
   175	
   176	        world.setVault(clanA, 100e18, 0, 100e18, 0);
   177	        world.setVault(clanB, 100e18, 0, 100e18, 0);
   178	        world.setVault(clanC, 100e18, 0, 100e18, 0);
   179	        world.setWallLevel(clanA, 1);
   180	        world.setWallLevel(clanB, 2);
   181	
   182	        _assertAllOk(_submitUpgradeBatch(elderA, clanA, 1));
   183	        _assertAllOk(_submitUpgradeBatch(elderB, clanB, 1));
   184	        _assertAllOk(_submitUpgradeBatch(elderC, clanC, 1));
   185	        _advanceTicks(world.getActionDuration(ActionType.UpgradeMonument) + 1);
   186	
   187	        (uint256 scoreA, uint64 reachedA, uint8 levelA) = world.getClanScore(clanA);
   188	        (uint256 scoreB, uint64 reachedB, uint8 levelB) = world.getClanScore(clanB);
   189	        (uint256 scoreC, uint64 reachedC, uint8 levelC) = world.getClanScore(clanC);
   190	        assertEq(scoreA, _expectedScore(levelA, reachedA, world.quoteLootValueRaw(clanA), 1), "A score");
   191	        assertEq(scoreB, _expectedScore(levelB, reachedB, world.quoteLootValueRaw(clanB), 2), "B score");
   192	        assertEq(scoreC, _expectedScore(levelC, reachedC, world.quoteLootValueRaw(clanC), 0), "C score");
   193	
   194	        (uint32[] memory ranked,) = world.getRankings();
   195	        assertEq(ranked[0], clanB, "highest wall wins after loot");
   196	        assertEq(ranked[1], clanA, "next wall second");
   197	        assertEq(ranked[2], clanC, "zero wall third");
   198	    }
   199	}
     1	// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
     2	pragma solidity ^0.8.34;
     3	
     4	import {Test} from "forge-std/Test.sol";
     5	import {ClanWorld} from "../src/ClanWorld.sol";
     6	import {
     7	    ClanWorldConstants,
     8	    ActionType,
     9	    ClansmanState,
    10	    StatusCode,
    11	    Clansman,
    12	    Mission,
    13	    ClanFullView,
    14	    ClanOrder,
    15	    OrderResult
    16	} from "../src/IClanWorld.sol";
    17	
    18	contract GatheringHarness is ClanWorld {
    19	    function setCarryWood(uint32 csId, uint256 wood) external {
    20	        _clansmen[csId].carryWood = wood;
    21	    }
    22	}
    23	
    24	contract GatheringTest is Test {
    25	    GatheringHarness world;
    26	    address elder = address(0xA1);
    27	
    28	    function setUp() public {
    29	        world = new GatheringHarness();
    30	    }
    31	
    32	    function _advanceTick() internal {
    33	        vm.warp(world.getWorldState().nextHeartbeatAtTs);
    34	        world.heartbeat();
    35	    }
    36	
    37	    function _advanceUntilCurrentTick(uint64 targetTick) internal {
    38	        while (world.getWorldState().currentTick < targetTick) {
    39	            _advanceTick();
    40	        }
    41	    }
    42	
    43	    function _mintClan() internal returns (uint32 clanId) {
    44	        vm.prank(elder);
    45	        (clanId,) = world.mintClan(elder);
    46	    }
    47	
    48	    function _firstCs(uint32 clanId) internal view returns (uint32) {
    49	        ClanFullView memory view_ = world.getClanFullView(clanId);
    50	        return view_.clansmen[0].clansman.clansman.clansmanId;
    51	    }
    52	
    53	    function _submitChopWood(uint32 clanId, uint32 csId) internal returns (OrderResult[] memory) {
    54	        ClanOrder[] memory orders = new ClanOrder[](1);
    55	        orders[0] = ClanOrder({
    56	            clansmanId: csId,
    57	            gotoRegion: ClanWorldConstants.REGION_FOREST,
    58	            action: ActionType.ChopWood,
    59	            targetClanId: 0,
    60	            marketToken: address(0),
    61	            marketAmount: 0,
    62	            maxGoldIn: 0
    63	        });
    64	
    65	        vm.prank(elder);
    66	        return world.submitClanOrders(clanId, orders);
    67	    }
    68	
    69	    function _settleChopWood(uint32 clanId, uint32 csId) internal returns (Clansman memory) {
    70	        OrderResult[] memory result = _submitChopWood(clanId, csId);
    71	        assertEq(uint8(result[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "chop wood accepted");
    72	
    73	        Mission memory mission = world.getActiveMission(csId);
    74	        _advanceUntilCurrentTick(mission.settlesAtTick + 1);
    75	        world.settleClan(clanId);
    76	        return world.getClansman(csId);
    77	    }
    78	
    79	    function test_chopWoodAtForestYieldsBaseTimesActionDuration() public {
    80	        vm.prevrandao(bytes32(uint256(2)));
    81	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan();
    82	        uint32 csId = _firstCs(clanId);
    83	
    84	        Clansman memory cs = _settleChopWood(clanId, csId);
    85	
    86	        uint256 expected = ClanWorldConstants.WOOD_YIELD_PER_TICK * world.getActionDuration(ActionType.ChopWood);
    87	        assertEq(cs.carryWood, expected, "base wood yield");
    88	    }
    89	
    90	    function test_chopWoodCritDistributionAcrossSeeds() public {
    91	        uint256 baseYield = ClanWorldConstants.WOOD_YIELD_PER_TICK * world.getActionDuration(ActionType.ChopWood);
    92	        uint256 critCount = 0;
    93	        world = new GatheringHarness();
    94	        uint256 cleanState = vm.snapshotState();
    95	
    96	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
    97	            assertTrue(vm.revertToState(cleanState), "reset gathering world");
    98	            vm.prevrandao(bytes32(uint256(i + 10_000)));
    99	            uint32 clanId = _mintClan();
   100	            uint32 csId = _firstCs(clanId);
   101	
   102	            Clansman memory cs = _settleChopWood(clanId, csId);
   103	            if (cs.carryWood == baseYield * 2) {
   104	                critCount++;
   105	            } else {
   106	                assertEq(cs.carryWood, baseYield, "non-crit yield");
   107	            }
   108	        }
   109	
   110	        assertGe(critCount, 3, "crit count too low");
   111	        assertLe(critCount, 20, "crit count too high");
   112	    }
   113	
   114	    function test_chopWoodClampsToCarryCap() public {
   115	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan();
   116	        uint32 csId = _firstCs(clanId);
   117	        world.setCarryWood(csId, ClanWorldConstants.CLANSMAN_CARRY_CAP - 1e18);
   118	
   119	        Clansman memory cs = _settleChopWood(clanId, csId);
   120	
   121	        assertEq(cs.carryWood, ClanWorldConstants.CLANSMAN_CARRY_CAP, "wood carry cap");
   122	    }
   123	
   124	    function test_chopWoodAppliesCooldownPostSettle() public {
   125	        uint32 clanId = _mintClan();
   126	        uint32 csId = _firstCs(clanId);
   127	
   128	        Clansman memory cs = _settleChopWood(clanId, csId);
   129	
   130	        assertEq(uint8(cs.state), uint8(ClansmanState.WAITING), "mission completed");
   131	        assertFalse(world.getActiveMission(csId).active, "mission inactive");
   132	        assertGt(cs.cooldownEndsAtTs, block.timestamp, "cooldown starts on settlement");
   133	    }
   134	}

   474	
   475	    function test_depositResources_woodCarryMovesToVaultAndClears() public {
   476	        (ClanWorldTestHarness harness, uint32 clanId, uint32 csId) = _setupHarness();
   477	        uint8 homeRegion = harness.getClan(clanId).baseRegion;
   478	        uint256 woodDelta = 5e18;
   479	
   480	        harness.setCarry(csId, woodDelta, 0, 0, 0);
   481	        uint256 vaultBefore = harness.getClan(clanId).vaultWood;
   482	
   483	        OrderResult[] memory r = _submitOrderHarness(harness, clanId, csId, homeRegion, ActionType.DepositResources);
   484	        assertEq(uint8(r[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "deposit order should be accepted");
   485	
   486	        Mission memory mission = harness.getActiveMission(csId);
   487	        assertEq(
   488	            mission.settlesAtTick,
   489	            mission.executesAtTick + harness.getActionDuration(ActionType.DepositResources),
   490	            "deposit settles after transfer duration"
   491	        );
   492	
   493	        _advanceTickHarness(harness);
   494	        _advanceTickHarness(harness);
   495	
   496	        assertEq(harness.getClan(clanId).vaultWood, vaultBefore + woodDelta, "vault wood receives carried wood");
   497	        assertEq(harness.getClansman(csId).carryWood, 0, "carry wood is cleared");
   498	    }
   499	
   500	    function test_depositResources_emptyCarryNoopsWithoutEvent() public {
   501	        (ClanWorldTestHarness harness, uint32 clanId, uint32 csId) = _setupHarness();
   502	        uint8 homeRegion = harness.getClan(clanId).baseRegion;
   503	
   504	        OrderResult[] memory r = _submitOrderHarness(harness, clanId, csId, homeRegion, ActionType.DepositResources);
   505	        assertEq(uint8(r[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "empty deposit order should be accepted");
   506	
   507	        vm.recordLogs();
   508	        _advanceTickHarness(harness);
   509	        _advanceTickHarness(harness);
   510	        Vm.Log[] memory logs = vm.getRecordedLogs();
   511	        bytes32 depositedTopic = keccak256("ResourcesDeposited(uint32,uint32,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint32)");
   512	
   513	        for (uint256 i = 0; i < logs.length; i++) {
   514	            assertTrue(logs[i].topics[0] != depositedTopic, "empty deposit emits no ResourcesDeposited event");
   515	        }
   516	
   517	        assertFalse(harness.getActiveMission(csId).active, "empty deposit still completes");
   518	    }
   519	
   520	    function test_depositResources_multipleTypesMoveTogether() public {
   521	        (ClanWorldTestHarness harness, uint32 clanId, uint32 csId) = _setupHarness();
   522	        uint8 homeRegion = harness.getClan(clanId).baseRegion;
   523	        uint256 woodDelta = 4e18;
   524	        uint256 ironDelta = 2e18;
   525	        uint256 fishDelta = 3e18;
   526	
   527	        harness.setCarry(csId, woodDelta, ironDelta, 0, fishDelta);
   528	        Clan memory beforeClan = harness.getClan(clanId);
   529	
   530	        OrderResult[] memory r = _submitOrderHarness(harness, clanId, csId, homeRegion, ActionType.DepositResources);
   531	        assertEq(uint8(r[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.OK), "deposit order should be accepted");
   532	
   533	        _advanceTickHarness(harness);
   534	        _advanceTickHarness(harness);
   535	
   536	        Clan memory afterClan = harness.getClan(clanId);
   537	        Clansman memory afterCs = harness.getClansman(csId);
   538	
   539	        assertEq(afterClan.vaultWood, beforeClan.vaultWood + woodDelta, "wood transferred");
   540	        assertEq(afterClan.vaultIron, beforeClan.vaultIron + ironDelta, "iron transferred");
   541	        assertEq(afterClan.vaultFish, beforeClan.vaultFish + fishDelta, "fish transferred");
   542	        assertEq(afterCs.carryWood, 0, "wood carry cleared");
   543	        assertEq(afterCs.carryIron, 0, "iron carry cleared");
   544	        assertEq(afterCs.carryFish, 0, "fish carry cleared");
   545	    }
   546	
   547	    function test_depositResources_requiresHomeRegion() public {
   548	        (ClanWorldTestHarness harness, uint32 clanId, uint32 csId) = _setupHarness();
   549	        uint8 homeRegion = harness.getClan(clanId).baseRegion;
   550	        uint8 nonHomeRegion = homeRegion == ClanWorldConstants.REGION_FOREST
   551	            ? ClanWorldConstants.REGION_MOUNTAINS
   552	            : ClanWorldConstants.REGION_FOREST;
   553	
   554	        OrderResult[] memory r = _submitOrderHarness(harness, clanId, csId, nonHomeRegion, ActionType.DepositResources);
   555	
   556	        assertEq(uint8(r[0].status), uint8(StatusCode.ERR_INVALID_REGION), "deposit must target home region");
   557	    }
   558	
   559	    function test_depositResources_eventHasCorrectDeltas() public {
   560	        (ClanWorldTestHarness harness, uint32 clanId, uint32 csId) = _setupHarness();
   561	        uint8 homeRegion = harness.getClan(clanId).baseRegion;
   562

   565	        OrderResult[] memory r = _submitOrderHarness(harness, clanId, csId, homeRegion, ActionType.DepositResources);

   572	    }

   574	    function test_quoteTravel_outOfBounds_returnsZero() public {
   575	        (uint8 ticks, bytes8 path) = world.quoteTravel(9, 1);
   576	        assertEq(ticks, 0, "out-of-range src should return 0 ticks");
   577	        assertEq(path, bytes8(0), "out-of-range src should return empty path");
   578	
   579	        (ticks, path) = world.quoteTravel(1, 9);
   580	        assertEq(ticks, 0, "out-of-range dst should return 0 ticks");
   581	        assertEq(path, bytes8(0), "out-of-range dst should return empty path");
   582	    }
   583	
   584	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   585	    // Test A: quoteTravel(9,9) — both out-of-bounds same-region, must return (0, bytes8(0))
   586	    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   587	
   588	    function test_quoteTravel_outOfBounds_sameRegion() public {
   589	        // Previously (9,9) escaped the > 8 guard (same-region early return fired first),
   590	        // returning (0, bytes8(uint64(9) << 56)) instead of (0, bytes8(0)).
   591	        (uint8 ticks, bytes8 path) = world.quoteTravel(9, 9);
   592	        assertEq(ticks, 0, "quoteTravel(9,9): travelTicks must be 0");
   593	        assertEq(path, bytes8(0), "quoteTravel(9,9): path must be bytes8(0), not a packed 9-region sentinel");
   594	    }

     1	import type { IConvexClient } from '@clan-world/shared/adapters';
     2	import type { IElderMemoryStore, IElderPeerInbox } from '@clan-world/agents/seams';
     3	import type { ElderId } from './types';
     4	
     5	export interface ComposeDeps {
     6	  convex: IConvexClient;
     7	  memory: IElderMemoryStore;
     8	  peerInbox: IElderPeerInbox;
     9	}
    10	
    11	export interface ComposeArgs {
    12	  elder: ElderId;
    13	  clanId: string;
    14	  tick: number;
    15	}
    16	
    17	/**
    18	 * Compose the per-tick situation block for a single Elder.
    19	 *
    20	 * Block sections (in order — Elder prompts depend on this ordering):
    21	 *
    22	 *   1. Identity reminder      — "You are Elder N of Clan X"
    23	 *   2. Tick state             — current tick + tick-epoch info
    24	 *   3. World snapshot summary — leaderboard-relevant slice
    25	 *   4. Clan view              — clan's own resources, regions, pending orders
    26	 *   5. Peer messages          — whispers in this Elder's inbox
    27	 *   6. Memory continuity      — keys saved last reasoning cycle
    28	 *
    29	 * Format is plain text (Markdown headings) so it pastes legibly into a Claude
    30	 * Code session. We deliberately do NOT include Convex internal IDs or other
    31	 * implementation noise — only fields the Elder needs to reason.
    32	 */
    33	export async function composeSituationBlock(
    34	  args: ComposeArgs,
    35	  deps: ComposeDeps,
    36	): Promise<string> {
    37	  const [snap, view, peers, memory] = await Promise.all([
    38	    deps.convex.getSnapshot(),
    39	    deps.convex.getClanFullView(args.clanId),
    40	    deps.peerInbox.inbox(),
    41	    deps.memory.snapshot(),
    42	  ]);
    43	
    44	  const lines: string[] = [];
    45	  lines.push(`# Tick ${args.tick} — Elder ${args.elder} (Clan ${args.clanId})`);
    46	  lines.push('');
    47	
    48	  lines.push('## Identity');
    49	  lines.push(
    50	    `You are Elder ${args.elder}, ruling Clan ${args.clanId}. You reason once per tick and ` +
    51	      `submit clan orders via the \`elder clan submit-orders\` CLI before the tick closes.`,
    52	  );
    53	  lines.push('');
    54	
    55	  lines.push('## Tick state');
    56	  lines.push(`- current tick: ${args.tick}`);
    57	  lines.push(`- tick started at (unix s): ${snap.tickEpoch.startedAt}`);
    58	  lines.push(`- tick duration: ${snap.tickEpoch.durationMs}ms`);
    59	  lines.push('');
    60	
    61	  lines.push('## World snapshot');
    62	  if (snap.regions.length === 0 && snap.clans.length === 0) {
    63	    lines.push('- (no world data yet — Convex stub or pre-genesis)');
    64	  } else {
    65	    lines.push(`- regions: ${snap.regions.length}`);
    66	    lines.push(`- clans: ${snap.clans.length}`);
    67	    for (const clan of snap.clans) {
    68	      lines.push(`  - ${clan.id} (${clan.name}) — treasury: ${clan.treasury}`);
    69	    }
    70	  }
    71	  lines.push('');
    72	
    73	  lines.push('## Your clan');
    74	  lines.push(`- id: ${view.clan.id}`);
    75	  lines.push(`- name: ${view.clan.name}`);
    76	  lines.push(`- treasury: ${view.clan.treasury}`);
    77	  lines.push(`- controlled regions: ${view.controlledRegions.length}`);
    78	  for (const r of view.controlledRegions) {
    79	    lines.push(`  - ${r.id} (${r.name})`);
    80	  }
    81	  lines.push(`- pending orders for next tick: ${view.pendingOrders.length}`);
    82	  for (const o of view.pendingOrders) {
    83	    lines.push(`  - ${o.kind}: ${JSON.stringify(o.payload)}`);
    84	  }
    85	  lines.push('');
    86	
    87	  lines.push('## Peer messages (whispers)');
    88	  if (peers.length === 0) {
    89	    lines.push('- (none)');
    90	  } else {
    91	    for (const p of peers) {
    92	      lines.push(`- [${p.sentAt}] from clan ${p.fromClanId} (tick ${p.tick}): ${p.message}`);
    93	    }
    94	  }
    95	  // Whispers visible via the convex-side feed (public/region-broadcast variant).
    96	  if (view.whispers.length > 0) {
    97	    lines.push('');
    98	    lines.push('## Public whispers (region/world feed)');
    99	    for (const w of view.whispers) {
   100	      lines.push(`- tick ${w.tick} from clan ${w.fromClanId} → ${w.toClanId}: ${w.text}`);
   101	    }
   102	  }
   103	  lines.push('');
   104	
   105	  lines.push('## Memory (continuity)');
   106	  const memKeys = Object.keys(memory).sort();
   107	  if (memKeys.length === 0) {
   108	    lines.push('- (empty — first tick, or post-clear bootstrap)');
   109	  } else {
   110	    for (const k of memKeys) {
   111	      lines.push(`- ${k}: ${memory[k]}`);
   112	    }
   113	  }
   114	  lines.push('');
   115	
   116	  lines.push('## Action');
   117	  lines.push(
   118	    'Reason about your clan\'s next move. Use `elder peer whisper`, `elder memory save`, and ' +
   119	      '`elder clan submit-orders` to act. The runner will heartbeat the chain after the settle ' +
   120	      'window — get your orders in before then.',
   121	  );
   122	
   123	  return lines.join('\n');
   124	}

docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3867:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14957:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:471-473, 1072-1093` — Winter starvation deaths are keyed off the current world winter flags while replaying historical ticks. If a clan starves during winter but is only settled or viewed after winter ends, both `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` replay those winter ticks with `winterActive == false` and skip the deaths entirely. That breaks both mutating settlement and derived getters. Suggested fix: derive winter status from the replayed `tick` itself, not from the current `_world.winterActive` snapshot.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3869:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14999:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:471-473, 1072-1093` — Winter starvation deaths are keyed off the current world winter flags while replaying historical ticks. If a clan starves during winter but is only settled or viewed after winter ends, both `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` replay those winter ticks with `winterActive == false` and skip the deaths entirely. That breaks both mutating settlement and derived getters. Suggested fix: derive winter status from the replayed `tick` itself, not from the current `_world.winterActive` snapshot.
docs/reviews/pr250-codereview-codex-5-4.md:3871:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:23:| H3 | `ClanWorld.sol:471-473, 1072-1093` | 5.4 = **1/5** | HIGH | **Winter starvation replay bug.** `_applyUpkeep` + `_simulateApplyUpkeep` read `_world.winterActive` (current flag) while replaying historical ticks. A clan that starved during a past winter but settled after winter ends will skip those deaths in both mutating settlement AND derived getters. Fix: derive winter status from the replayed `tick`, not the current world flag. |

If a clan starves during winter but is only settled or viewed after winter ends, both `_applyUpkeep()` and `_simulateApplyUpkeep()` replay those winter ticks with `winterActive == false` and skip the deaths entirely. docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14975:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:1501-1508` — `_simulateCompleteMission()` writes `cooldownEndsAtTs` from `block.timestamp`, so derived getters are wall-clock-sensitive rather than tick-deterministic. Two reads in the same tick can return different cooldowns.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14999:- `packages/contracts/src/ClanWorld.sol:471-473, 1072-1093` — Winter starvation deaths are keyed off the current world winter flags while replaying historical ticks. docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:23:| H3 | `ClanWorld.sol:471-473, 1072-1093` | 5.4 = **1/5** | HIGH | **Winter starvation replay bug.** `_applyUpkeep` + `_simulateApplyUpkeep` read `_world.winterActive` (current flag) while replaying historical ticks. A clan that starved during a past winter but settled after winter ends will skip those deaths in both mutating settlement AND derived getters. Fix: derive winter status from the replayed `tick`, not the current world flag. |

   302	        // Reconstruct path backwards
   303	        uint8[8] memory path;
   304	        uint256 pathLen;
   305	        uint8 cur = dst;
   306	        while (cur != src) {
   307	            path[pathLen++] = cur;
   308	            cur = parent[cur];
   309	        }
   310	        path[pathLen++] = src;
   311	
   312	        // Reverse into result
   313	        bytes8 packed;
   314	        uint64 byteShift = 56;
   315	        for (uint256 i = pathLen; i > 0; i--) {
   316	            packed = packed | bytes8(uint64(path[i - 1]) << byteShift);
   317	            if (byteShift >= 8) byteShift -= 8;
   318	        }
   319	        return packed;
   320	    }
   321	
   322	    function _travelTicks(uint8 fromRegion, uint8 toRegion) private pure returns (uint8) {
   323	        if (fromRegion == 0 || toRegion == 0) return 0; // NOOP region
   324	        if (fromRegion == toRegion) return 0;
   325	        if (fromRegion > 8 || toRegion > 8) return 0;
   326	        return _distMatrix(fromRegion, toRegion);
   327	    }
   328	
   329	    function _addTicksClamped(uint64 tick, uint64 delta) private pure returns (uint64) {
   330	        if (type(uint64).max - tick < delta) return type(uint64).max;
   331	        return tick + delta;
   332	    }
   333	
   334	    // =========================================================================
   335	    // INTERNAL SETTLEMENT
   336	    // =========================================================================
   337	
   338	    /// @dev Settle a single clansman's mission for the tick range [fromTick, toTick).
   339	    ///      Handles all 6 mission lifecycle paths. Called from _settleClan and settleClansman.
   340	    function _settleMissionForClansman(
   341	        Clan storage clan,
   342	        Clansman storage cs,
   343	        uint32 clanId,
   344	        uint64 fromTick,
   345	        uint64 toTick
   346	    ) internal {
   347	        Mission storage m = _missions[cs.clansmanId];
   348	
   349	        // Path 6: dead clansman — invalidate active mission if any
   350	        if (cs.state == ClansmanState.DEAD) {
   351	            if (m.active) {
   352	                if (m.action == ActionType.DefendBase) {
   353	                    _clearDefender(cs.clansmanId);
   354	                } else if (m.action == ActionType.UpgradeWall) {
   355	                    _refundWallUpgradeReservation(cs.clansmanId);
   356	                } else if (m.action == ActionType.UpgradeBase) {
   357	                    _refundBaseUpgradeReservation(cs.clansmanId);
   358	                } else if (m.action == ActionType.UpgradeMonument) {
   359	                    _refundMonumentUpgradeReservation(cs.clansmanId);
   360	                }
   361	                m.active = false; // silent invalidation; dead clansman gets no MissionCompleted
   362	            }
   363	            return;
   364	        }
   365	
   366	        if (!m.active) return; // no active mission — nothing to settle
   367	
   368	        bytes32 tickSeed;
   369	        for (uint64 tick = fromTick; tick < toTick; tick++) {
   370	            tickSeed = _tickSeeds[tick];
   371	
   372	            // Path 1 → Path 2 transition: TRAVELING → ACTING at arrivalTick
   373	            if (cs.state == ClansmanState.TRAVELING && tick >= m.arrivalTick) {
   374	                cs.state = ClansmanState.ACTING;
   375	                cs.currentRegion = m.targetRegion;
   376	                emit WorkerArrived(clanId, cs.clansmanId, m.targetRegion, tick);
   377	
   378	                if (m.action == ActionType.DefendBase) {
   379	                    _registerDefender(m.targetRegion, clanId, cs.clansmanId);
   380	                }
   381	            }
   382	
   383	            if (m.action == ActionType.DefendBase) continue; // persistent defender mission
   384	
   385	            // Path 3: ACTING at/past settlesAtTick → resolve
   386	            if (cs.state == ClansmanState.ACTING && tick >= m.settlesAtTick) {
   387	                _resolveAction(clan, cs, m, clanId, tick, tickSeed);
   388	                if (m.active && getActionDuration(m.action) > 0) {
   389	                    _completeMission(cs, m);
   390	                }

  1554	    function test_phase3E7_missionOverwrite_nonceIncrements_interruptedEmitted() public {

codex
I found one suspicious cross-phase change outside the buildings scope: the PR also rewrites wood gathering constants and carry cap. I’m pulling the authoritative spec text around those values now, because if that wasn’t an approved Phase 8 change it’s exactly the kind of silent economy regression we want to catch before merge.

docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:- v4.3 §K: *"`ERR_CARRY_FULL` is especially relevant for explicit market buy validation… requested output cannot fit remaining carry capacity"*
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:24:The buy path is **internally contradictory** — it gates on worker carry capacity but mutates vault, so the carry-cap check no longer reflects what the action actually does. Practical consequences: a worker with full carry but an empty vault is rejected from buying; a worker physically present at UT with carry to sell is irrelevant — only the clan's homebase vault is consulted (vault is at the homebase, far from UT).

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5678:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:11:The phase ships ~85% of the bandit subsystem (state machine, spawn, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup). All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:5685:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6171:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:16:- v4.3 §K: *"`ERR_CARRY_FULL` is especially relevant for explicit market buy validation… requested output cannot fit remaining carry capacity"*
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6172:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:24:The buy path is **internally contradictory** — it gates on worker carry capacity but mutates vault, so the carry-cap check no longer reflects what the action actually does. Practical consequences: a worker with full carry but an empty vault is rejected from buying; a worker physically present at UT with carry to sell is irrelevant — only the clan's homebase vault is consulted (vault is at the homebase, far from UT).

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6274:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6275:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6716:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1548:+        assertEq(cs.carryWood, ClanWorldConstants.CLANSMAN_CARRY_CAP, "wood carry cap");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6853:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3680:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:6856:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3710:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:11:The phase ships ~85% of the bandit subsystem (state machine, spawn, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup). All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7195:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:5623:docs/reviews/pr200-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6338:/bin/bash -lc "rg -n \"mintClan\\(|goldBalance|blueprintBalance|clanState = ClanState.DEAD|clanState == ClanState.DEAD|_poolForResourceType|seedPools\\(|transferGold\\(|transferVaultResource\\(|transferBlueprint\\(|transferBundle\\(|settleClansman\\(|DepositResources|CLANSMAN_CARRY_CAP|WOOD_YIELD_PER_TICK|ResourceMinted|ResourceBurned\" packages/contracts/src packages/contracts/test" in /home/claude/code/clan-world
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7325:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6081:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7326:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6082:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7364:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6257:docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-opus-4-7.md:24:The buy path is **internally contradictory** — it gates on worker carry capacity but mutates vault, so the carry-cap check no longer reflects what the action actually does. Practical consequences: a worker with full carry but an empty vault is rejected from buying; a worker physically present at UT with carry to sell is irrelevant — only the clan's homebase vault is consulted (vault is at the homebase, far from UT).
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-4.md:7411:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6524:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3680:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6524:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:6534:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-gemini-3-1-pro.md:464:VERDICT: **NEEDS_FIXES**. While the Phase 9 PR successfully integrates foundational spawn chance logic and deterministic attack resolution, it suffers from critical architectural drift regarding the bandit state machine and eager-settlement scope. Bandits lack automated transitions to select targets or recover from escapes, rendering the threat entirely inert in practice. Additionally, wall defense calculations and view-only settlement simulations contain edge cases that undermine base progression and UI accuracy.

docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7245:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:11:The phase ships ~85% of the bandit subsystem (state machine, spawn, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup). All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7598:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1514:+        assertEq(cs.carryWood, expected, "base wood yield");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:7601:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:1533:+                assertEq(cs.carryWood, baseYield, "non-crit yield");
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8185:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3680:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.
docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:8196:docs/reviews/pr199-codereview-codex-5-5.md:3710:docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:11:The phase ships ~85% of the bandit subsystem (state machine, spawn, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup). All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.

docs/conventions/gitflow.md:16:1. **Create issue.** Title is imperative ("Add foundry loop heartbeat"). Body has acceptance criteria + agent-dispatchable scope.
docs/conventions/hackathon-rules.md:18:- A few critical error cases get a test each — only the ones that would corrupt state or silently lose user input. Examples that qualify: missing required env var, malformed onchain payload, Convex mutation rejected by schema. Examples that do NOT qualify: every possible enum branch, every off-by-one, every type-coercion edge.
docs/conventions/hackathon-rules.md:49:// GOOD — one happy path, one critical error
docs/reference/prize-strategy.md:3:Two stacked submissions. Different audiences, different judging criteria.

docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-codex-5-4.md:14460:   249	**Assert:** At least one randomized outcome differs (e.g. crit, wound chance). Confirms seed is actually wired into resolution logic.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:11:The phase ships ~85% of the bandit subsystem (state machine, spawn, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, target-death cleanup). All five reviewers independently flagged the SAME critical gap: **production bandits never attack**. They spawn → Camped → STUCK FOREVER. All the attack/loot/blueprint code is dead in the live heartbeat path; only test harnesses force the Camped→Attacking transition.
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:66:- **Opus 4.6:** NEEDS_FIXES — 1 HIGH (seed timing, theoretical) + 4 MED + 3 LOW (single HIGH was less critical than other models found)
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-synthesis.md:72:- Findings flagged by 4 models: **1** (Camped→Attacking — the most critical)
docs/reviews/r2/pr194-codereview-opus-4-7.md:6:**NEEDS_FIXES.** The phase ships a mostly-coherent bandit subsystem, but it has a critical architectural gap: bandits **never actually attack in production**. There is code for spawn, camp transition, attack resolution, defender split, blueprint reward, and target-death cleanup — but nothing in `heartbeat()` ever transitions a Camped bandit into the Attacking state, so all of `_resolveAttackingBandits` / `_resolveBanditAttack` / loot / blueprint / cleanup logic is unreachable from a normal heartbeat. The defender registry, simulation parity, seed timing, and arithmetic all look sound otherwise. Recommend a single follow-up fix to wire Camped→Attacking dispatch (or explicit acknowledgment that Wave-N attack dispatch is deferred to a sub-issue) before merging.

docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10907:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).
docs/reviews/pr198-codereview-codex-5-4.md:10939:  Market I/O is using the wrong balance domain. Sells read/deduct `clan.vault*`, and buys credit `clan.vault*`, while submission still rejects buys based on worker carry capacity. Per the Phase 6 market spec, `market_sell` is from worker carry and `market_buy` returns to worker carry. As shipped, a clansman can liquidate home-vault inventory without hauling it to Unicorn Town, and bought goods become instantly eligible for upkeep/bandit theft without a deposit trip. The tests lock this in (`packages/contracts/test/ClanWorld.t.sol:775-888, 1013-1085, 1485-1490`).

    // Derived read getters (read-only simulation forward to current tick)

    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------

    function getDerivedClanState(uint32 clanId) external view returns (DerivedClanState memory);

    function getBanditTargetPreview(uint32 banditId) external view returns (uint32 previewClanId);

    function quoteTravel(uint8 srcRegion, uint8 dstRegion) external view returns (uint8 travelTicks, bytes8 path);

    function quoteLootValueRaw(uint32 clanId) external view returns (uint256 lootValue);

    function quoteLootValueSettled(uint32 clanId) external view returns (uint256 lootValue);

    // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    // UI indexer aggregator getters (v4.4 additions)
    //
    // These are pure compositions of the derived getters above and exist only
    // to reduce indexer RPC traffic. They MUST follow the same non-mutation
    // rule as the underlying derived getters.
